subject: Ecology
title: Endemism
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Endemism.

hook.text: An endemic species lives in one place and nowhere else on Earth. Islands are full of them.

buildup.text: Isolation drives endemism—species evolve unique traits with no gene flow from outside.

discovery.text: Madagascar has over 10,000 endemic plant species. Ninety percent of its wildlife is found only there.

twist.text: High endemism means high vulnerability—lose the island and you lose those species globally.

climax.text: Islands cover 5% of Earth's land but hold 20% of its bird, reptile, and plant species.

punchline.text: Found here and nowhere else. That's the risk.

quiz.question: What does endemic mean in ecology?
options: Found in one location and nowhere else
